strategy: Hit with Ice (+50% incoming); avoid Fire (80% resist).
Do not use Ice, Fire, or universal Blades unprotected. He steals them off the receiver. Set up with traps and Feint instead, or Aegis your one key blade.
Skip universal shields and single-target Absorbs too; he steals those wards as well.
He is only 4860 health and stunable, so burst him down well before round 4 to dodge his Frost Giant, and use a stun if you need to skip a turn.
Blade (Aegis-protected) or trap, Feint, then one big Ice hit.
cheatNotes: Casting a universal Shield or single-target Absorb makes him steal that ward instantly. Skip tower/absorb setup or Aegis it.
Multi-target universal shields like Legion Shield only lose the caster's copy, so allies keep theirs.
Any Ice, Fire, or universal Blade gets stolen off the receiver. Use traps and Feint instead, or Aegis the blade.
A multi-target universal blade like Bladestorm only loses the caster's copy; teammates keep theirs.
Aegis on the blade or ward blocks the steal entirely. Aegis your one key blade before the hit.
Pet may-casts and pet spells also trigger the steals, so watch pet blades.
At the end of round 4 and every 8th round after he casts Frost Giant (Ice AoE). Race him down before then.
At round 8 he casts an enhanced Fire Dragon for 495 plus 395 over three rounds; you should be long done by then.
After his big Fire hits he may drop a Fire Prism on a random wizard and natural-attack them; a Fire Prism on you only matters if you plan to hit Fire.
If this triggers while he has no pips, his natural attack still hits as though he had one pip.
The natural attack from this cheat can still be Dispelled; a Fire Dispel on Billy blanks it.
